mpeg_o.providers.sqlite
=======================

.. py:module:: mpeg_o.providers.sqlite

.. autoapi-nested-parse::

   SQLite storage provider — Milestone 41 stress-test of Provisional ABCs.

   Stores the full MPEG-O group/dataset tree in a single SQLite file.
   Groups and datasets are rows in relational tables; primitive dataset
   data is stored as little-endian BLOBs; compound datasets are stored as
   JSON arrays of row-dicts.

   This implementation deliberately uses no external dependencies beyond
   the Python standard library and numpy — sqlite3 ships with CPython.

   Schema version: 1
   Provider identifier: mpeg_o.providers.sqlite

   API status: Provisional (stress-test — not for production use yet).

   SPDX-License-Identifier: LGPL-3.0-or-later



Classes
-------

.. autoapisummary::

   mpeg_o.providers.sqlite.SqliteDataset
   mpeg_o.providers.sqlite.SqliteGroup
   mpeg_o.providers.sqlite.SqliteProvider


Module Contents
---------------

.. py:class:: SqliteDataset(conn: sqlite3.Connection, dataset_id: int, name: str, precision: mpeg_o.enums.Precision | None, shape: tuple[int, Ellipsis], fields: tuple[mpeg_o.providers.base.CompoundField, Ellipsis] | None, read_only: bool = False)

   Bases: :py:obj:`mpeg_o.providers.base.StorageDataset`


   A dataset row backed by a SQLite ``datasets`` table entry.

   Primitive datasets store data as a little-endian BLOB.
   Compound datasets store rows as a JSON array of dicts.


   .. py:property:: name
      :type: str



   .. py:property:: precision
      :type: mpeg_o.enums.Precision | None


      Element precision for primitive datasets; ``None`` for
      compound datasets (use :meth:`compound_fields` there).


   .. py:property:: shape
      :type: tuple[int, Ellipsis]


      Full shape tuple. 1-D datasets return ``(N,)``.


   .. py:property:: chunks
      :type: tuple[int, Ellipsis] | None


      Chunk shape, or ``None`` for contiguous storage. Default
      ``None`` — providers that care override.


   .. py:property:: compound_fields
      :type: tuple[mpeg_o.providers.base.CompoundField, Ellipsis] | None


      Field schema for compound datasets, or ``None`` for
      primitive datasets.


   .. py:method:: read(offset: int = 0, count: int = -1) -> numpy.ndarray

      Read ``count`` elements starting at ``offset``.

      For primitive datasets returns a numpy array.
      For compound datasets returns a list of dicts (not a structured
      ndarray) — the provider stores JSON, not numpy-structured bytes,
      so a full structured ndarray cannot be reconstructed without the
      full round-trip encoding overhead.  This is an **interface gap**
      noted in the report.



   .. py:method:: write(data: Any) -> None

      Write the full dataset.

      For primitive datasets, ``data`` must be array-like; for compound
      datasets, ``data`` must be a list of dicts.



   .. py:method:: has_attribute(name: str) -> bool


   .. py:method:: get_attribute(name: str) -> Any


   .. py:method:: set_attribute(name: str, value: Any) -> None


   .. py:method:: attribute_names() -> list[str]


   .. py:method:: delete_attribute(name: str) -> None


   .. py:method:: close() -> None

      No-op — connection is owned by the provider.



.. py:class:: SqliteGroup(conn: sqlite3.Connection, group_id: int, name: str, read_only: bool = False)

   Bases: :py:obj:`mpeg_o.providers.base.StorageGroup`


   A row in the ``groups`` table, exposed as a StorageGroup.


   .. py:property:: name
      :type: str



   .. py:method:: child_names() -> list[str]


   .. py:method:: has_child(name: str) -> bool


   .. py:method:: open_group(name: str) -> SqliteGroup


   .. py:method:: create_group(name: str) -> SqliteGroup


   .. py:method:: delete_child(name: str) -> None


   .. py:method:: open_dataset(name: str) -> SqliteDataset


   .. py:method:: create_dataset(name: str, precision: mpeg_o.enums.Precision, length: int, *, chunk_size: int = 0, compression: mpeg_o.enums.Compression = Compression.NONE, compression_level: int = 6) -> SqliteDataset

      Create a primitive 1-D dataset.

      ``chunk_size == 0`` selects a contiguous (unchunked) layout.
      ``compression == NONE`` disables filters regardless of
      ``compression_level``.



   .. py:method:: create_dataset_nd(name: str, precision: mpeg_o.enums.Precision, shape: tuple[int, Ellipsis], *, chunks: tuple[int, Ellipsis] | None = None, compression: mpeg_o.enums.Compression = Compression.NONE, compression_level: int = 6) -> SqliteDataset

      Create a multi-dimensional dataset. 1-D path delegates to
      :meth:`create_dataset` for backward compat; overrides extend
      to higher ranks.



   .. py:method:: create_compound_dataset(name: str, fields: list[mpeg_o.providers.base.CompoundField], count: int) -> SqliteDataset

      Create a 1-D compound dataset with the given field schema.



   .. py:method:: has_attribute(name: str) -> bool


   .. py:method:: get_attribute(name: str) -> Any


   .. py:method:: set_attribute(name: str, value: Any) -> None


   .. py:method:: delete_attribute(name: str) -> None


   .. py:method:: attribute_names() -> list[str]


   .. py:method:: close() -> None

      No-op — connection is owned by the provider.



.. py:class:: SqliteProvider

   Bases: :py:obj:`mpeg_o.providers.base.StorageProvider`


   SQLite-backed storage provider.

   Each MPEG-O file is a single ``.mpgo.sqlite`` SQLite database.

   Usage::

       p = SqliteProvider()
       p.open("/path/to/data.mpgo.sqlite", mode="w")
       root = p.root_group()
       ...
       p.close()

   Or as a context manager::

       with SqliteProvider.open("/path/to/data.mpgo.sqlite", mode="r") as p:
           ...

   URL form ``sqlite:///abs/path/to/data.mpgo.sqlite`` is also
   accepted by :meth:`open`.

   API status: Provisional (stress-test).
   SPDX-License-Identifier: LGPL-3.0-or-later


   .. py:method:: open(path_or_url=None, *, mode: str = 'r', **kwargs) -> SqliteProvider

      Open or create a SQLite backing store.

      Supports two call styles:

      *Instance style* (mutates self, return value = self)::

          p = SqliteProvider()
          p.open("/path/to/data.mpgo.sqlite", mode="w")

      *Class style* (creates new instance)::

          p = SqliteProvider.open("/path/to/data.mpgo.sqlite", mode="w")

      ``mode`` semantics:

      * ``"r"``        — read-only; file must exist.
      * ``"r+"``/``"rw"`` — read/write; file must exist.
      * ``"w"``        — create or truncate.
      * ``"a"``        — read/write; create if absent.

      Implementation note: this method intentionally does NOT use
      ``@classmethod`` so that the instance-mutating call style works.
      When called as ``SqliteProvider.open(path)``, Python passes the
      path string as the first positional argument; we detect this via
      ``isinstance`` and construct a new instance accordingly.



   .. py:method:: close() -> None


   .. py:property:: provider_name
      :type: str


      Short identifier used for logging and registry lookup
      (``"hdf5"``, ``"memory"``, ``"zarr"``, …).


   .. py:method:: is_open() -> bool


   .. py:method:: root_group() -> SqliteGroup


   .. py:method:: native_handle() -> sqlite3.Connection | None

      Return the raw ``sqlite3.Connection``. Escape hatch.



   .. py:method:: supports_url(url: str) -> bool
      :staticmethod:


      Return True for ``sqlite://`` URLs and recognised file extensions.



   .. py:method:: __repr__() -> str
